title: Causes, Herbal Treatments, And Preparations For Over Bleeding During Periods
causes_desc: When it comes to heavy periods, there are several reasons why it might be happening. Let's break it down. One main cause is hormonal imbalance. Your hormones, specifically estrogen and progesterone, play a crucial role in regulating your menstrual cycle. If these hormones get out of balance, it can cause your uterus to prepare for a pregnancy even when you're not pregnant, leading to thicker uterine lining and heavier bleeding.  Another reason for over bleeding is having an IUD, or intrauterine device, inserted. The IUD is a small device that's put inside your uterus to prevent pregnancy. While it's a safe and effective method of birth control, it can sometimes cause heavier periods in the first few months after insertion. This is because the IUD can cause your uterus to release more blood and tissue, leading to heavier bleeding.  Age can also play a role in over bleeding. As you get older, your periods may become heavier due to the natural decline in estrogen levels. This decline can cause your uterus to thin out, leading to heavier bleeding.  PCOS, or polycystic ovary syndrome, is another condition that can cause heavy periods. PCOS is a hormonal disorder that affects ovulation and can cause cysts on your ovaries. Women with PCOS often have higher levels of androgens, which can disrupt their menstrual cycles and lead to heavier bleeding.  Lastly, uterine fibroids are growths in your uterus that can cause heavy periods. These growths can put pressure on your uterus, causing it to release more blood and tissue, leading to heavier bleeding. Fibroids can also cause cramping and discomfort during your period.
herbs_desc: Herbs like Angelica sinensis, also known as Dong Quai, are traditionally used to help with heavy menstrual bleeding. It has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling in the uterus and improve blood flow. This can help the body to regulate and control bleeding.  Glycyrrhiza glabra, or Licorice root, is often used to balance and stabilize hormone levels. Hormonal imbalances can cause irregular periods and heavy bleeding. Licorice root can help to regulate these hormone levels, which in turn can help to reduce bleeding.  Cimicifuga racemona, or Black Cohosh, is another herb that can help to regulate hormone levels. It's particularly helpful for women who experience heavy bleeding due to hormonal imbalances. Black Cohosh can also help to reduce inflammation and ease cramps.  Paeonia lactiflora, or Peony, is a herb that's been used for centuries to help with menstrual issues. It has anti-inflammatory properties and can help to reduce bleeding by improving blood flow and regulating hormone levels. Peony can also help to reduce anxiety and stress related to menstrual issues.  Trifolium pratense, or Red Clover, is high in isoflavones, which can help to balance hormone levels and reduce inflammation. This can help to regulate periods and reduce heavy bleeding. Red Clover can also help to ease symptoms of menopause and reduce hot flashes.  These herbs can be used in combination to help alleviate heavy menstrual bleeding. However, it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies, especially if you have any underlying medical conditions or are taking medications.
preparations_desc: When you're experiencing heavy bleeding during your periods, herbal preparations can be a natural and effective way to help manage the symptoms. A decoction, made by simmering herbs in water, can be especially helpful. For example, the roots of the dandelion plant contain compounds that can help reduce inflammation and ease menstrual cramps. You can make a decoction by simmering the roots in water for 5-10 minutes.  Another way to use herbs is through an infusion, where you steep the herbs in hot water to make a tea. Chamomile, for instance, has a soothing effect on the body and can help calm menstrual cramps and anxiety. To make an infusion, simply steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried chamomile flowers in a cup of hot water for 5-10 minutes.  You can also use herbal teas to help with heavy bleeding. Raspberry leaf tea, for example, is rich in iron and can help reduce menstrual bleeding. To make a tea, simply steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried raspberry leaves in a cup of hot water for 5-10 minutes.  Tinctures, which are concentrated herbal extracts, can also be used to help manage menstrual symptoms. A tincture of the herb shepherd's purse, for example, can help reduce menstrual bleeding and ease cramps. To use a tincture, simply add a few drops to a glass of water and drink it as needed.  Finally, you can also take herbal supplements in capsule form. A capsule of the herb vitex, for example, can help regulate menstrual cycles and reduce heavy bleeding. To use a capsule, simply take it as directed on the label, usually 1-2 capsules per day.
benefits_desc: Using herbs to help with heavy periods can be really helpful for some women. These herbs can ease the pain and discomfort that comes with over bleeding. They can also help to reduce the amount of bleeding and make it more manageable. Some herbs can help to slow down the blood flow, which can give the body a chance to heal and stop the bleeding. They can also help to calm the uterus and reduce inflammation, which can cause pain and discomfort. Additionally, some herbs have properties that can help to balance the body's hormones, which can contribute to heavy periods. This can help to regulate the menstrual cycle and reduce the risk of over bleeding. By using these herbs, women can find relief from the pain and discomfort of heavy periods, and get back to their normal routine.
